source.notes:
Lead source: inbound from app partner (sales enablement/call-summaries marketplace).

CRM-style summary: brief, pragmatic SMB — 25 employees, US sales org, high call volume (~500 calls/mo across team). Currently uses in-house Node app to stitch Zoom transcriptions; manual redaction and QA steps take ~6-8 hours/wk. Goal: reduce manual post-call work; auto-generate 3-5 bullet summaries + action items; flag PII.

PII & retention concerns (customer quotes):
- 'We can't have raw transcripts stored for more than 7 days.'
- 'Need a simple way to redact or avoid storing email/cc numbers in records.'
- 'If we use hosted API, can we ensure transcripts are ephemeral and not used for model training?'

What they care about: latency (wants <1.5s/token for streaming summary), pricing predictability (they run small but frequent jobs), simple SDK and drop-in integration for Node, minimal infra overhead. Prefers public cloud hosted offering (self-serve).

Technical notes from SE call:
- Prefer model variants that balance cost and quality (suggest Redwood's small-to-medium models).
- Want support for prefix caching to avoid re-summarizing repeat snippets.
- Asking about a mute/redact preprocessor SDK hook (client-side redaction) — suggests a checkbox in onboarding.

POC ask: trial keys for dev team, test data sample (50 calls), 30-day retention / option to disable storage, docs on retention controls + audit logs.

Objections/risks: budget sensitive (wants <$500/mo cap), uncertain about compliance (needs SOC2 evidence), worries about accidental PII persists in logs.

Next steps (short):
- Send tailored hosted-api trial config & quickstart Node sample (Maya) — include client-side redaction example.
- Attach security FAQ + SOC2 cert stub (drive link).
- Confirm retention options: ephemeral processing, 0-day storage flag, plus paid audit logs add-on.
- Schedule 30-min follow-up week of 2026-03-22 to review POC results.

Stubs/links: Fireflies ff_20260304_6753, ff_20260310_8431; Gmail threads thread_1874623/1875867; Drive deck + security notes.

Sales notes: tone = fast/technical; ideal close motion = PLG self-serve trial -> add-on billed card; work to convert to monthly commitment if POC shows 40-50% time savings.

source.notes:
Inbound SMB trial. Fast signup; self-serve. Created first key but usage stalled after smoke test. Key facts:
- Primary contact: Thomas Reed (CTO, small engineering team).
- Use-case: searchable product docs + in-app support assistant. Wants embeddings + chat routing as next step.
- Latency target: 200-350ms p95 for short chat flows; throughput low (10-30 qps peak).
- Cost sensitivity: high — looking to minimize per-token spend; asked about batching + prefix caching.
- Model preference: open models OK if cost/latency competitive; asked about Redwood-verified low-cost variants.
- Security: basic SOC2 assurance acceptable for now; will escalate to SSO/SAML if they move to Dedicated.
Challenges/observations:
- After initial SE call, no real requests beyond healthcheck. Likely cause: team started evaluating OpenAI due to aggressive free trial credits and ecosystem plugins.
- Quote from Thomas on 2026-03-09: "we can’t justify a switch until we see $/q improvement or simpler SDKs — send a sample cost calc".
- They requested a short batching example and a sample cost projection; we sent docs (drive link above) but no follow-up.
Next steps recommended:
- Quick, personalized ROI email showing concrete $/1000-queries comparison with configured batching + caching scenarios.
- Offer a 2-week trial top-up or credit to run embeddings job (their blocker).
- Book 30-min troubleshooting seat with Marcus to help run the first embeddings pipeline.
- If no re-engagement by 2026-03-20, mark as at-risk churn and add to winback cadence.
Stale signals: trial expiry set to auto-expire 2026-03-15 if no activity (system).

notes: Inbound SMB lead. self-serve sign-up, exploring hosted API for chat + recommendations.
Primary ask: "how to pick between Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants for our support use-case" - care about accuracy on product Q&A and hallucination rates.
Throughput/latency targets: target 10-30 qps steady, 80 qps short bursts; median p95 latency target <300ms for responses under 500 tokens.
Cost sensitivity: want to keep monthly inference spend < $3k during pilot. asking for per-1k-token cost estimates for 7B vs 13B hosted variants.
Model questions noted on call: quantized models ok? tradeoffs for int8/in4 vs fp16 on hallucination and response quality; memory and cold-start effects.
They run many multi-turn conversations with context ~3-5 turns; interested in prefix caching and KV reuse - asked if Redwood supports automatic KV cache across sessions.
Asked about fallback policies: prefer route to cheaper 7B (Mistral/Qwen) for low-confidence answers, escalate to 13B for high-confidence or critical flows.
Wants deterministic latency: asked about per-model median/p95 numbers and regional routing (NA). prefers single-region public cloud for now.
Security: SOC2 required before prod; SSO/SAML for admin console; audit log retention 90 days.
POC plan: 2-week pilot (chat + embeddings), compare Mistral-7B-instruct vs Llama-2-13B, measure F1/EM on 200 prompts sample set + latency/cost. goal: decide model mix for GA.
Quote from eng lead: "If we can get comparable quality at 40-50% cost using Mistral-7B with caching/fallbacks, we'll choose that."
Open asks for Redwood: sample cost calculator for our tokens profile, example rollout policy to fallback between models, recommended prompt templates for Mistral vs Llama.
Action items: send multicase cost comparison (estimates for streaming vs non-streaming), share eval harness template and quickstart notebook; enable trial credits and add SE to account.
Other artifacts: provided drive pricing deck link, mailed compliance FAQ (cc'd).

notes: SMB dev tool startup building in-app AI features (chat first, later embeddings for search).
Primary channel: self-serve signups -> want quick test in prod sandbox. Cost-sensitive but willing to pay for reliable UX.
Traffic profile: small baseline (3-8 rps) with short promos causing spikes up to ~120 rps for 20-40s. Typical burst pattern last quarter during newsletter sends.

Key ask: how does Redwood handle 429s? Must include Retry-After header or backoff strategy.

Security: SSO/SAML for admin console, audit logs for usage, data residency not required (public cloud ok).
Pricing sensitivity: estimated spend ~ $300-$1200/mo depending on token usage and how burst billing is assessed.
Requested artifacts: sample app that simulates 100->150 rps bursts, docs on quotas/limits, example backoff code, one-pager on burst billing.

source.notes:
Inbound from product: building a lightweight CX automation layer for small e-commerce merchants. Primary ask = hosted API (self-serve).
- Found us via blog post on structured output + tool-calling. Signed up for free credits 2026-03-01.
- Use-case: automatic ticket summarization + structured JSON output to map to ticket fields (category, priority, order_id). Also want tool-calling to patch/update tickets in Zendesk and run triage rules.
- Perf targets: p50 summary latency < 300ms for short messages, burst throughput ~20-40 requests/sec during peak; cost sensitive (tight margin for SMB customers).
- Model prefs: prefer Redwood-verified smaller models (13B/quantized) or composite routing: low-cost base for summaries, higher-quality model for escalations. Want auto-fallback to cheaper model when capacity/cost constraints.
- Security: SOC2 required before procurement; need SSO/SAML for admin console; audit logs for ticket edits; retention controls for customer transcripts.
- Quote from CTO (Maya): "We need predictable latency — if hosted path can't hit sub-300ms for short summaries, our agents will see it as a regression."
- POC scope proposed: 2-week hosted API trial — 1) wire up summary endpoint into their ingestion pipeline, 2) implement structured output schema and test mapping to Zendesk ticket fields, 3) test tool-calling flow to update ticket meta (status, tags).
- Success metrics: 30% reduction in avg handle time, >85% accuracy mapping to ticket category, <$0.0008/token target cost for summaries.
- Current stack: Node.js microservices, PostgreSQL for ticket store, Zapier connector for 3rd-party apps. They want simple SDK and minimal infra changes.
- Internal champions: Maya Lin (CTO), Ben Ortiz (Head of Product). AE notes: good technical buyer, will involve ops for SOC2 later.
- Risk: SMB budget cycle monthly but legal turnaround can be slow for DPA. Concern about tokenization and PII handling; asked for KMS/HSM info (we have drive link).

title: SignalMoss CX
preview:
Inbound from product: building a lightweight CX automation layer for small e-commerce merchants. Primary ask = hosted API (self-serve).
- Found us via blog post on structured output + tool-calling. Signed up for free credits 2026-03-01.
- Use-case: automatic ticket summarization + structured JSON output to map to ticket fields (category, priority, order_id). Also want tool-calling to patch/update tickets in Zendesk and run triage rules.
- Perf targets: p50 summary latency < 300ms for short messages, burst throughput ~20-40 requests/sec during peak; cost sensitive (tight margin for SMB customers).
- Model prefs: prefer Redwood-verified smaller models (13B/quantized) or composite routing: low-cost base for summaries, higher-quality model for escalations. Want auto-fallback to cheaper model when capacity/cost constraints.
- Security: SOC2 required before procurement; need SSO/SAML for admin console; audit logs for ticket edits; retention controls for customer transcripts.
- Quote from CTO (Maya): "We need predictable latency — if hosted path can't hit sub-300ms for short summaries, our agents will see it as a regression."
- POC scope proposed: 2-week hosted API trial — 1) wire up summary endpoint into their ingestion pipeline, 2) implement structured output schema and test mapping to Zendesk ticket fields, 3) test tool-calling flow to update ticket meta (status, tags).
- Success metrics: 30% reduction in avg handle time, >85% accuracy mapping to ticket category, <$0.0008/token target cost for summaries.
- Current stack: Node.js microservices, PostgreSQL for ticket store, Zapier connector for 3rd-party apps. They want simple SDK and minimal infra changes.
- Internal champions: Maya Lin (CTO), Ben Ortiz (Head of Product). AE notes: good technical buyer, will involve ops for SOC2 later.
- Risk: SMB budget cycle monthly but legal turnaround can be slow for DPA. Concern about tokenization and PII handling; asked for KMS/HSM info (we have drive link).

source.notes:
Lead source: inbound via blog post 'building better RAG for docs'. Small dev-forward SaaS building a searchable knowledge base for SMB customers. Wants hosted API for self-serve POC — low friction.

Key requirements / quotes:
- Workload: embeddings + retriever + reranker pipeline. Primary traffic: user-initiated doc search (web UI) ~100k queries/month.
- Latency target: median query RTT <300ms (embedding + retrieval + reranker).
- Cost sensitivity: high — needs predictable per-query pricing. CTO: "we need predictable per-query cost — we can't absorb wild token spikes".
- Model preferences: open models preferred; interested in Redwood catalog and guidance on quantized variants for cost.
- Vector DB: currently on Supabase; asks for examples / best practice for integrating Redwood hosted API with Supabase and when to use Redis/Weaviate alternatives.
- Reranking: evaluating single-call reranker vs two-step (embedding retrieval then lightweight reranker). Wants sample code and recommended latency/cost tradeoffs.
- Security/compliance: SSO/SAML for team, audit logging required, US-only data residency requested; SOC2 on roadmap.

POC plan: ingest 1.2M tokens (product docs + user guides), run 2-week relevance test vs current BM25 approach, measure p@1/p@3 and latency. Needs trial credits & quota bump.

Stubs/refs: Fireflies ff_20260301_auriga_call_42, drive/folders/1Auriga-Pricing-FAQ, thread-17902-auriga-inbound, ticket RWD-1452.

Sales notes: prioritize clear cost examples for 100k q/mo, provide Supabase integration sample, deliver reranker pattern doc and one-page POC playbook. Blockers: legal DPA + retention defaults; SE needed for deeper vector DB tuning.

notes: Inbound SMB lead via self-serve signup — triggered support chat after first POC run.
Primary contact: Diego Ramos (Head of Product). Wants fast ramp, low ops.
Use-case: customer support assistant that calls backend tools (ticket lookup, reassign) and returns strict JSON for downstream UI rendering.
Tried: Node SDK streaming + functions. Observed: last chunk sometimes contains trailing partial JSON -> client-side parser fails.
Observed: when tool-calling, runtime returns parameter names that don't match our function schema (camelCase vs snake_case) -> automatic validator throws.
Quote from Diego: 'We need deterministic JSON output so our React UI can map fields without extra parsing hacks.'
Cost sensitivity: medium — SMB budget; prefers hosted API to avoid infra.
Desired latency: <250ms for interactive chat median; burst to 40 rps during support peaks.
Model preference: open model family ok; wants recommended small/fast variant for cost-sensitive paths and fallback to higher-quality for escalations.
Security: SSO/SAML required for admin console; audit logs for tool calls in POC.
They attempted JSON_MODE with 'response_format': 'json_schema' and saw two classes of failure: 1) streaming boundary truncation; 2) function param shape mismatch when calling external ticketing tool.
Asked for: code snippet that shows robust streaming parser pattern + server-side example showing how to emit final newline/close-token to avoid truncation.
AE notes: candidate for hosted_api self-serve but high chance to convert within quarter if demo resolves streaming issues.
Reference artifacts attached below (drive/pricing/security).

Internal: SE Maya to replicate on sandbox key and open SDK bug if repro consistent. Possible quick fix: ensure final event emits complete JSON 'done' envelope or use built-in structured output enforcement in platform.
Next call: 2026-03-10 10:00 PT — demo of patched streaming client + function schema suggestion doc.
Pipeline: move to pilot if POC passes parsing and function-call audit logging.
Notes from support chat: 'sometimes the tool call returns extra meta field "_call_id" and our schema validator errors out' — likely param naming mismatch.
They requested example mapping layer or middleware recommendation for snake_case <-> camelCase normalization as stopgap.

source.notes:
Snapshot from AE/SE interactions and security review. POC never started — fell apart during security and resourcing alignment. Key points:
- 2025-11-04 inbound lead via trade show; initial discovery call (Ava Chen + Diego). Company running camera analytics across 12 facilities, wants near-real-time event classification and operator summarization.
- They insist on private deployment inside their VPC or fully on-prem for 3 'critical' sites. KMS control mandatory; FIPS and export considerations flagged.
- Security sent initial questionnaire (see linked doc). They flagged: no third-party KMS allowed unless HSM integration; 3-year retention + audit export requirements.
- SE calls 2025-12-03 demo + architecture walkthrough. Ironwood liked runtime performance claims but asked for proof of KMS/HSM integration and proof of audit log retention.
- Procurement then announced a company-wide cap on external SaaS spend (2026-01). POC budget deferred.
- Security lead (Marisol Vega) resigned in Jan 2026 during internal reorg; no replacement named. Internal champion evaporated.
- Competitor (local systems integrator) promised an on-prem appliance with delegated KMS and a short pilot on their pre-approved partner contract; procurement gravitated there due to pre-existing vendor relationship.
- AE notes: "They were the most security-sensitive prospect we've seen in this vertical — wanted explicit HSM certs and an onsite install option."
- POC status: never kicked off. Could not agree on success criteria (what counts as usable classification at 95%+ across camera types) and who funds engineering time.
- Current disposition: stalled/closed-lost (soft). Sales recommended leaving in nurture w/ quarterly check-ins; security FAQ updates to be shared if we add HSM integration details.

source.notes:
Inbound self-serve sign-up via hosted API trial. SMB web agency building marketing assistants that call internal tools (pricing lookup, ticket creation). 

Lead source and context:
- signed up with company email 2025-11-02, used free credits to run quick prototypes
- submitted support thread reporting streaming / function-calling issues 2025-11-06

Summary of current problem (customer reporting):
- Using streaming responses with structured JSON mode + functions (tool-calling). Expected: continuous chunked JSON building then final function call. Actual: intermittent malformed JSON chunks causing client-side JSON.parse failures.
- Example error reported: "JSONParseError: Unexpected token '}' in stream chunk" and "InvalidFunctionArgs: parameter 'price' expected number but got string".
- Customer paraphrase in call: "The stream looks right in the console but our frontend chokes when a chunk arrives with a partial object — we end up with trailing commas / missing braces."

What we've checked so far (timeline):
- 2025-11-03: customer built minimal prompt and function schema, hit early parse errors while using dev chrome client.
- 2025-11-04: email thread with dev sample (attached curl + schema). Gmail thread id: 1782abcd.
- 2025-11-06: SE Ethan replicated parse error in Redwood hosted sandbox using customer's schema; confirmed mismatch on numeric vs string types in schema for price and quantity. Fireflies call id for triage: ff_20251106_inkbloom.
- 2025-11-07: Support engineer created internal ticket to capture streaming chunk order and newline separator behavior (JIRA: RBW-1932).
- 2025-11-08: Quick fix suggested — normalize types on the client (coerce strings to numbers) and add server-side streaming buffer until full JSON object received. Customer tried but wants robust server-side solution.
- 2025-11-10: 30m discovery call with AE + SE + devs. Agreed next step: Redwood SE to produce a reproducible curl + minimal model call that demonstrates the malformed-chunk scenario and a recommended server-side reassembly pattern.

Technical details / notes for SE handoff (copied from customer samples):
- Function schema excerpt provided by customer (they used JSON Schema-like structure): {
  "name": "create_ticket",
  "parameters": {"type": "object", "properties": {"title": {"type": "string"}, "price": {"type": "number"} } }
}
- Customer payloads sometimes send price as "12.99" (string) from their ecommerce tool — leads to InvalidFunctionArgs.
- Streaming behaviour: they are using client-side incremental JSON.parse per-chunk. When the model yields escaped newlines or when a chunk boundary splits a unicode escape, parse fails.
- They attempted to set "json_mode": "strict" (their term) but unclear which SDK flag they used; need to map to Redwood streaming structured output flags and example.

Customer constraints / priorities:
- Wants low-friction fix (prefer server-side guidance vs heavy client changes).
- Cost conscious: initial ARR target small; prefers conservative token budgets during repro.
- Timebox: wants working demo by end of Nov to include in client pilot.

Suggested action items (internal):
- SE (Ethan) to craft minimal repro (curl + small prompt + functions) that reproduces chunked-malformed JSON on hosted_api streaming mode. Include both correct and incorrect examples.
- Provide recommended client pattern: buffer/concatenate chunks until a full JSON object can be parsed, use content-length-like heuristics or explicit chunk delimiters when possible. Provide code snippet for Node: stream collector + try/except JSON.parse.
- Create short KB note for SMB self-serve: common structured-output pitfalls (type coercion, chunk boundary parsing, stop sequences that truncate trailing braces). Link from support.
- Confirm whether Redwood server can optionally enforce type coercion or emit canonical types in function arguments when model generates e.g., "12.99" -> 12.99. If not, document requirement clearly.

Customer quotes (verbatim-ish):
- "We love the responsiveness of the hosted API but the stream parsing is the only blocker for production."
- "If you can give a one-page snippet for our frontend team we can test in the morning."

Linked artefacts:
- Fireflies id: ff_20251106_inkbloom
- Gmail thread id: 1782abcd
- Drive doc (pricing + trial notes): https://drive.redwood/InkBloom/onboarding-pricing-v1 (customer shared view-only)
- Jira: RBW-1932 (internal support ticket capturing raw stream logs)

Next meeting: schedule 30m debug session week of 2025-11-17 (tentative).
